request: How do I stop my overnight coding agent from burning through $400 of API calls grinding on a task it's never actually going to finish?
rationale: Unattended agent, budget cap, and no-progress / stop-condition design. Core loop-engineering territory even though the word 'loop' is never used.

request: Our maker-checker pipeline keeps approving bad outputs. The checker is just another call to the same model with the same prompt. How do I make the review step actually catch the bad ones?
rationale: Verifier fidelity and maker/checker independence in a repeated review pipeline. Directly the skill's spine.

request: We've got a queue of about 5,000 records and an agent that fixes each one, re-validates, retries on failure, and moves on. It clears 4,900 fine but spins forever on a handful. How should I structure this so it doesn't get stuck?
rationale: Queue-processing agent that retries/revises items against a validator, with a stuck-item / no-progress problem. Squarely in scope.

request: Write me a Python script that dumps my Postgres database and uploads the backup to S3 every night on a cron schedule.
rationale: Ordinary deterministic automation: no agent, no attempt/verify/revise loop. A named non-fit. Sanity check that the obvious case does not over-fire.

request: I have a deterministic ETL pipeline: extract from a vendor API, transform, load to the warehouse, hourly. Occasionally a row fails schema validation and I want it to retry that row up to 3 times before skipping. How should I add the retry?
rationale: The real boundary test: 'retry on validation failure' sounds like the queue/verify loop, but this is deterministic code with simple retries, no agent and no revise-against-feedback. Not named in the description, so it tests whether the boundary generalizes. Over-firing here means the description is too broad on 'retry/queue'.

request: I want to run 10 agents in parallel, each researching a different competitor, then stitch their write-ups into one report. How should I wire that up?
rationale: Parallel fan-out with a final merge, no iterative revision of items against feedback. The commonly-confused case; the description excludes 'parallel agents that don't iteratively revise against feedback.'
